summary.intent: Investigate and fix GitHub issue #452 in the moltis repo where OpenClaw skills with human-readable names (containing spaces/special chars) were being silently excluded from the manifest due to name validation failures.
summary.outcome: Implemented a slug fallback mechanism in the skills parsing pipeline, added 7 new tests, updated all SkillMetadata construction sites across 9 files, and committed/pushed the fix with a PR.

repo: SkillMetadata in crates/skills/src/types.rs had no slug or display_name fields, even though _meta.json (SkillMetaJson) already supported both fields — they just weren't wired into parsing
validate_name() enforces slug-format names (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens + colons) and was silently excluding skills with human-readable names
scan_repo_skills in install.rs was logging at debug! level for non-conforming SKILL.md files, making the bug hard to diagnose
The _meta.json fallback path (read_meta_json) existed but was only used in the gateway service layer, not during manifest parsing
Skills crate has construction sites spread across: discover.rs, formats.rs, registry.rs, requirements.rs, prompt_gen.rs, agents/src/prompt.rs, chat/src/lib.rs

code.finding: parse_metadata() validated meta.name with validate_name() with no fallback — root cause of the bug
SkillMetaJson already had slug and display_name fields but they were never consulted during parse_metadata() or scan_repo_skills()
SkillMetadata struct had no slug or display_name fields; added both as Option<String> and derived Default
scan_repo_skills loop logged at debug! level for non-conforming SKILL.md — should be warn! with actionable detail

workflow: Deriving Default on a struct with all Option/Vec/String fields enables ..Default::default() spread syntax across many construction sites, avoiding tedious field-by-field updates
Adding optional fields with #[serde(default)] to a widely-constructed struct still requires updating all struct literal sites in Rust — no way around it without Default derive
Checking downstream crate compilation separately (agents, chat) after fixing the core crate is a necessary step when struct definitions change

friction: Many SkillMetadata struct literal construction sites spread across multiple crates required individual updates — a mechanical but error-prone process
Missed one construction site in discover.rs on first pass, requiring a second compile-fix cycle
Had to check whether SkillMetadata could safely derive Default before using ..Default::default() spread syntax
open_items: install.rs:306-323 scan_repo_skills loop should be upgraded from debug! to warn! logging for non-conforming SKILL.md files — mentioned in analysis but not implemented
openclaw-import/src/skills.rs was read but no changes were described — may also need updating to handle slug/display_name fields consistently
The _meta.json slug fallback path was added as a second fallback in resolve_name_or_slug(), but it's unclear if this path is tested under all edge cases
